observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal Accounting Office and Municipal Engineer’s Office implements a monthly reconciliation process where all ongoing and completed projects are reviewed to ensure timely capitalization of assets into the PPE accounts. This should involve the submission of completion certificates and asset verification to ensure all completed projects are capture.

We recommended and Management agreed to the following:
The Municipal Accounting Office ensure the proper recording of all purchases and issuances of semi-expendable property under the appropriate inventory accounts, in compliance with COA Circular Nos. 2022-004 and 2024-006;
The Municipal Accounting Office and the General Services Office maintain the prescribed inventory records, including the Semi-Expendable Property Ledger Card, Registry of Semi-Expendable Property Issued, and related reports, to enhance property monitoring and control; and
The GSO conduct periodic physical inventories of semi-expendable property and submit the Report on the Physical Count of Semi-Expendable Property to the audit team in accordance with reporting deadlines and COA guidelines.

We recommended and Management agreed to the following:
Ensure the full implementation of corrective actions already initiated by finalizing the reversion and reclassification of all stale checks; and
The Municipal Accountant prepare the corresponding journal entries to reflect the reclassification of all stale checks in the accounting records, and to update the Bank Reconciliation Statement by removing the stale checks from the outstanding list.

We recommended and Management agreed to the following:
The Municipal Accountant, in coordination with the Municipal Treasurer, immediately conduct a full reconciliation of the RPT and SET receivable accounts;
All adjustments made to RPT and SET receivable accounts must be properly documented with clear explanations, supporting schedules, and source documents to ensure accuracy and traceability; and
Implement monthly or quarterly reconciliation procedures between the Accounting and Treasury Offices to ensure consistency and accuracy of reported balances. These reconciliations should be formally documented and reviewed by supervisory officials.

We recommended and Management agreed that the  Municipal Accountant institute the necessary adjustments in the accounting records to rectify the erroneous classifications by debiting the appropriate Property, Plant, and Equipment (PPE) accounts and crediting the Prior Period Adjustment account, in accordance with applicable accounting standards and policies.

The Municipal Treasurer deposit her collections regularly and intact and discontinue the practice of retaining/withholding part of her collections pursuant to Section 69(4) of P. D. 1445 and Section 32 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs, Volume I, to ensure the safety and avoid risk of loss of government funds.

The Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant should regularly reconcile their records to adjust/correct the difference in the Cash-Local Treasury account and errors could be rectified at the earliest possible time.
The LDRRMO review and update annually the LDRRMFIP to include activities to be funded from the unexpended QRF and DRRMF-MOOE of the previous years which were transferred to the Special Trust Fund.

Prioritize the development of GAD PPAs that are realistic and attainable to ensure that direct, immediate and substantial benefits are provided to the public; 

Regularly monitor/review the GAD budget if it is being spent for what it was intended for and check on the actual cost of implementing the GAD activities to avoid over allocation; and

Strengthen the monitoring and evaluation system to secure the effective and efficient implementation of GAD GPB.

Consider attribution of the Municipality’s major programs to the GAD budget subject to Harmonized Gender and Development Guidelines (HGDG) assessment tool to maximize utilization of GAD Budget.

Direct the GAD Focal Person to expedite the setting up and maintenance of the GAD database to appropriately identify gender issues and provide basis for gender-responsive planning, programming and policy formulation; and	
	
Direct the Monitoring and Evaluation Team to conduct annual monitoring of the accomplishments and evaluation of the utilization and outcome of the GAD budget and prepare the form prescribed in Item H of the JMC No. 2013-01 of the PCW, DILG, DBM and NEDA.

To appropriate only those programs/ projects/ activities enumerated under DepEd-DILG-DBM Joint Circular No. 1 s. 2017 for Special Education Fund and avoid charging unrelated expenses which could defeat the purpose for which the fund was created.

The Local Chief Executive ensure the timely submission of properly accomplished action plan on the CY 2023 AAR findings and recommendations including that of the prior years and submit the same to the audit team; and 

The Management shall faithfully observe the 60-day period within which to submit to the audit team from receipt of the succeeding AARs, either in printed form or by way of electronic document, a status report on the actions taken on said audit findings and recommendations.

The Management agreed to direct the LDRRM Council to decide on the use of the unexpended balance of the LDRRMF.  Review and update annually the LDRRMF Investment Plan to incorporate the PPAs to be funded from the unexpended QRF and DRRMF-MOOE of the previous years to facilitate effective monitoring of the fund.

The Municipal Treasurer deposit her collections regularly and intact and discontinue the practice of retaining/withholding part of her collections pursuant to Section 69(4) of P. D. 1445 and Section 32 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs, Volume I, to ensure the safety and avoid risk of loss of government funds; and

The Solid Waste Management Office continuously coordinate with the concerned Barangays to prioritize and facilitate the establishment of functional MRFs as required under section 32 of Republic Act No. 9003 to help achieve the Municipality’s commitment towards sustainable development and implementation of an environmentally sound and safe management of its solid waste.
The Municipality should make an ordinance to impose proper segregation of waste from source.

Direct the Municipal Planning and Development Officer to spearhead the establishment of the GAD Database to gather information such as gender statistics and sex disaggregated data to serve as inputs for planning, budgeting, programming and policy formulation; and
The management exert effort to have its Solid Waste Management Plan for 2018-2027 approved by the National Solid Waste Management Commission.

We recommended and the Management agreed to direct the LDRRM Council to decide on the use of the unexpended balance of the LDRRMF.  Henceforth, review and update annually the LDRRMF Investment Plan to incorporate the PPAs to be funded from the unexpended QRF and DRRMF-MOOE of the previous years to facilitate effective monitoring of the fund.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the Solid Waste Management Office continuously coordinate with the concerned Barangays to prioritize and facilitate the establishment of functional MRFs as required under section 32 of Republic Act No. 9003 to help achieve the Municipality’s commitment towards sustainable development and implementation of an environmentally sound and safe management of its solid waste.
The Municipality should make an ordinance to impose proper segregation of waste from source.

Direct the Municipal Planning and Development Officer to spearhead the establishment of the GAD Database to gather information such as gender statistics and sex disaggregated data to serve as inputs for planning, budgeting, programming and policy formulation; and
The management exert effort to have its Solid Waste Management Plan for 2018-2027 approved by the National Solid Waste Management Commission.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal Treasurer deposit her collections regularly and intact and discontinue the practice of retaining/withholding part of her collections pursuant to Section 69(4) of P. D. 1445 and Section 32 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs, Volume I, to ensure the safety and avoid risk of loss of government funds.
We further recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant should regularly reconcile their records to adjust/correct the difference in the Cash-Local Treasury account and errors could be rectified at the earliest possible time.

We recommended and the Management agreed to the following:
The Municipal Accountant evaluate the need for those unexpended fund balances from NGAs, if the funds be proven to be no longer needed, these should be returned to the source agencies or else transfer the balances from TF to GF as prescribed in Section 3.2 of the COA Circular 2012-001;
The Municipal Treasurer’s Office prepare the disbursement voucher for the refund of the unutilized funds for return to the SAs once the funds be proven to be no longer needed; and
Henceforth, the management monitor the status of funds and the implementation of the programs/projects and promptly submit the liquidation reports and documents for a refund of the unutilized balance.

The Municipal Development Council (MDC) and the budget committee, during the preparation of annual budget for 20% Development Fund, include, PPAs which are only feasible and could be accomplished within the target date of completion, after taking into consideration the priorities of the LGU leadership. 

The Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator (MPDC) and the Municipal Engineer’s Office strictly observe the schedule of implementation in accordance with Annual Investment Plan; and

The MPDC, the Municipal Engineer, the Municipal Budget Officer, and the Municipal Accountant closely monitor the implementation of the different development programs, projects and activities as reflected in the Annual Investment/Development Plan to avoid additional cost as a result of delayed implementation.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the HRMO require the preparation and submission of monthly individual accomplishment report and assess the periodic performance of JO personnel to ensure value for money and avoid wastage of government funds.  The same should be attached to the payroll submitted to the audit team to validate their salary claims.

a.  Management direct the LDRRM Council to decide on the use of the unexpended balance of the LDRRMF, and review and update annually the LDRRMF Investment Plan to incorporate the PPAs to be funded from the unexpended QRF and DRRMF-MOOE of the previous years so that the Municipality becomes a disaster resilient community; and

b.  The Municipal Accountant shall take into account the unexpended balance of the QRF and DRRMF-MOOE of the current year and previous years within the five-year validity period of the STF in order to record the correct balance of Trust Liabilities-DRRMF account and to fairly present the Municipality’s financial statements.

The Municipal Mayor directs the Municipal Accountant to file a request for write-off of the receivable account balances which were considered dormant and could no longer be collected in accordance to COA Circular 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.

We recommended and the Management agreed to direct the LDRRM Council to decide on the use of the unexpended balance of the LDRRMF, and review and update annually the LDRRMF Investment Plan to incorporate the PPAs to be funded from the unexpended QRF and DRRMF-MOOE of the previous years so that the Municipality becomes a disaster resilient community.

We recommended and the Management agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to file a request for write-off of the receivable account balances which were considered dormant and could no longer be collected in accordance to COA Circular 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.

We recommended and the Management agreed to the following:
The Municipality should transport the waste to MRF as soon as possible; and
The Municipality should make an ordinance to impose proper segregation of waste from source.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal Treasurer conducts intensive tax campaign, employ collection strategies, and institute legal remedies pursuant to pertinent provisions of RA 7160 in order to persuade the Taxpayers to pay their tax on time.

observations.recommendation:
a.	Strictly follow the prescribed procedures enumerated Chapter 5 (b) of the NGAS Manual  for LGUs, Volume 1 in the recording of disbursements in the Trust Fund; and

b.	Book - up in the General Fund the amount of ₱13.230 million PPEs acquired in CY 2020 and all those PPEs acquired under the Trust Fund in prior years in order to correctly and fairly present these accounts in the financial statements.

a.  Direct the Municipal Accountant and Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to submit the required documents to the Office of the Auditor for further evaluation of the transactions pursuant to Section 4(6) of Presidential Decree No. 1445 and GPPB Circular No. 01-2020 dated April 6, 2020; and

b.  Require the BAC to strictly comply with the posting requirement of GPPB Circular No. 01-2020 dated April 6, 2020 for information purposes and to promote transparency and accountability in the conduct of Emergency Procurement under the Bayanihan Act.

a.  Direct the Municipal Planning and Development Officer to spearhead the establishment of the GAD Database to gather information such as gender statistics and sex disaggregated data to serve as inputs for planning, budgeting, programming and policy formulation; and

b.  Ensure that adequate budget is provided for the establishment and maintenance of the GAD Database.

Encourage the JO and contractual personnel to register as SSS self-employed members in adherence to the provisions of DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2014-83 in order to avail of the benefits and protection against hazards and disability, sickness, maternity, old age, death and other contingencies that the SSS offers.

The Municipal Accountant request from the depository banks of the Municipality of Madalag the Credit Memorandum, and in case of inaction, the request should be elevated to the Head Office of the bank.
The management exert effort to have its Solid Waste Management Plan for 2018-2027 approved by the National Solid Waste Management Commission.

The Solid Waste Management Office continuously coordinate with the concerned Barangays to prioritize and facilitate the establishment of functional MRFs as required under section 32 of Republic Act No. 9003 to help achieve the Municipality’s commitment towards sustainable development and implementation of an environmentally sound and safe management of its solid waste.

The provisions of the HGDG be complied with by subjecting the GAD PPAs to the GAD Checklist during the project identification and designing programs to determine whether the same fully address gender related issues.

The Municipal Mayor issue an Executive Order creating the Monitoring and Evaluation Team to perform such functions as enumerated under Section 5 of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01.

The Solid Waste Management Board implement immediately the acquisition of ECO-SWM Site and cause the closure of existing open dump site pursuant to Sections 17(h) and 37 of RA 9003 and closure order of the Department of Environment and Natural Resources.  As an alternative, sanitary landfill sites shall be developed and operated as a final disposal site for solid and, eventually, residual wastes in compliance with the Solid Waste Management Act.

The Municipal Treasurer shall send at once confirmation letters to all payees of checks that remained outstanding to date to determine the actual status of the provincial’s obligation to them and copy furnished this Office of the same.
observations.reason:
The Municipal Treasurer was not able to send confirmation letters.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the following and the Management agreed to require the Municipal Accountant to:
Strictly follow the prescribed procedures enumerated Chapter 5 (b) of the NGAS Manual  for LGUs, Volume 1 in the recording of disbursements in the Trust Fund; and
Book - up in the General Fund the amount of ₱13.230 million PPEs acquired in CY 2020 and all those PPEs acquired under the Trust Fund in prior years in order to correctly and fairly present these accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended the following and the Management agreed to:
Direct the Municipal Accountant and Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to submit the required documents to the Office of the Auditor for further evaluation of the transactions pursuant to Section 4(6) of Presidential Decree No. 1445 and GPPB Circular No. 01-2020 dated April 6, 2020; and
Require the BAC to strictly comply with the posting requirement of GPPB Circular No. 01-2020 dated April 6, 2020 for information purposes and to promote transparency and accountability in the conduct of Emergency Procurement under the Bayanihan Act.

We recommended the following and the Management agreed to:
Management direct the LDRRM Council to decide on the use of the unexpended balance of the LDRRMF, and review and update annually the LDRRMF Investment Plan to incorporate the PPAs to be funded from the unexpended QRF and DRRMF-MOOE of the previous years so that the Municipality becomes a disaster resilient community; and
The Municipal Accountant shall take into account the unexpended balance of the QRF and DRRMF-MOOE of the current year and previous years within the five-year validity period of the STF in order to record the correct balance of Trust Liabilities-DRRMF account and to fairly present the Municipality’s financial statements.

We recommended the following and the Management agreed to:
Direct the Municipal Planning and Development Officer to spearhead the establishment of the GAD Database to gather information such as gender statistics and sex disaggregated data to serve as inputs for planning, budgeting, programming and policy formulation; and
Ensure that adequate budget is provided for the establishment and maintenance of the GAD Database.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal Mayor directs the Municipal Accountant to file a request for write-off of the receivable account balances which were considered dormant and could no longer be collected in accordance to COA Circular 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.

We recommended and the management agreed to encourage the JO and contractual personnel to register as SSS self-employed members in adherence to the provisions of DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2014-83 in order to avail of the benefits and protection against hazards and disability, sickness, maternity, old age, death and other contingencies that the SSS offers.

Reconciliation of the RPCPPE be made by the Municipal Supply/Property Unit and the Accounting Office.  Considering the huge amount and quantity to be reconciled, develop strategies, policies and procedures so that reconciliation of PPE can me made easier in order that the noted discrepancy be minimized, if not eliminated; and

Investigate and clear all discrepancies between the physical count and accounting records.  If necessary, require written explanation from the persons liable.

We recommended that the Municipal Accounting and Municipal Treasurer’s Offices strictly comply with BIR RMC No. 23-2012 on the full remittance of taxes withheld within the prescribed period to avoid penalties/surcharges.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer strictly comply with the regulations on the submission of the DVs and their supporting documents and RCDs and their official receipts to the COA Audit Team to avoid delays in the verification and conduct of other audit-related activities.

The Municipal Accountant request from the depository banks of the Municipality of Madalag the Credit Memorandum, and in case of inaction, the request should be elevated to the Head Office of the bank.

The Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer submit the Disbursement Vouchers and their supporting documents and Report of Collections and Deposits and their Official Receipts within the reglementary period to this Office.
The management exert effort to have its Solid Waste Management Plan for 2018-2027 approved by the National Solid Waste Management Commission.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the following:
Reconciliation of the RPCPPE be made by the Municipal Supply/Property Unit and the Accounting Office.  Considering the huge amount and quantity to be reconciled, develop strategies, policies and procedures so that reconciliation of PPE can me made easier in order that the noted discrepancy be minimized, if not eliminated; and
Investigate and clear all discrepancies between the physical count and accounting records.  If necessary, require written explanation from the persons liable.

We recommended the following:
Comply strictly with the rules and regulations as provided in Section 89 of PD 1445 and COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997 in the grant, utilization and liquidation of cash advances;
Monitor strictly the liquidation of unliquidated cash advances; and
Before signing/approving the clearances of employees, management official should diligently check their respective records of accountability to avoid incurrence of unliquidated cash advance.

We recommended that the Municipal Accounting and Municipal Treasurer’s Offices strictly comply with BIR RMC No. 23-2012 on the full remittance of taxes withheld within the prescribed period to avoid penalties/surcharges.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer strictly comply with the regulations on the submission of the DVs and their supporting documents and RCDs and their official receipts to the COA Audit Team to avoid delays in the verification and conduct of other audit-related activities.

We recommended that the management:
Strictly observed the schedule of implementation in accordance with the Annual Investment Plan;
Mobilize the concerned offices in the early planning and implementation of development projects to avoid incurring possible additional cost as a result of delayed implementation; and
Submit justifications for the non-implementation and delayed completion of the above-mentioned priority projects totaling P14.900 million.

We recommended that the Management must take necessary action on the settlement of the Notices of Suspensions, Notices of Disallowance and Notices of Charge as of December 31, 2019 in the total amount of P5,396.00.

The Inventory Committee conduct an inventory of the local roads under the jurisdiction of the municipality and render a Report on the Physical count of the Road Networks System.

The Municipal General Service Officer or its equivalent:

Render a Report on Local Road Network and Road Map

Regularly maintain and update the Registry of Public Infrastructures for Road

Keep a complete Local Road Network Property Card for all roads and its components
The Municipal Engineer provide the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal General Service Officer with the complete description and cost segregation of road components for road projects

The Municipal Accountant:

Prepare the JEV to record the beginning balance of the local road network and its components

Prepare and maintain a Local Road Network Ledger Card

Keep and maintain subsidiary records for roads and road components for every road network

Prepare a lapsing schedule for the computation of the depreciation for each component at the end of the year.

The management immediately implement the programs, projects and activities particularly those comprising the 70% of the LDRRM Fund order to achieve the objectives of the LDRRM and submit justification for their non-implementation during the budget year.

The Solid Waste Management Board implement immediately the acquisition of ECO-SWM Site and cause the closure of existing open dump site pursuant to Sections 17(h) and 37 of RA 9003 and closure order of the Department of Environment and Natural Resources.  As an alternative, sanitary landfill sites shall be developed and operated as a final disposal site for solid and, eventually, residual wastes in compliance with the Solid Waste Management Act.

observations.recommendation:
The management must firmly require collectors to turnover their collections to the municipal treasurer daily and intact pursuant to Section 29 of the Manual on the NGAS for LGUs, Volume I and Section 26 of COA Circular No.92-382 in order to avoid the possibilities of misuse, misapplication or the ultimate loss of government funds. 

Collectors who failed to abide with the above stated regulation should be dealt with accordingly. Disciplinary actions or sanctions should be imposed when necessary in order to enforce compliance with the existing rules and regulations on collection, reporting and remittances and in order to safeguard government funds.

Collectors who failed to abide with the above stated regulation should be dealt with accordingly. Disciplinary actions or sanctions should be imposed when necessary in order to enforce compliance with the existing rules and regulations on collection, reporting and remittances and in order to safeguard government funds.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the following:

The reconciliation of the RPCPPE be made by the Municipal Supply/Property Unit and the Accounting Office.  Considering the huge amount and quantity to be reconciled, develop strategies, policies and procedures so that reconciliation of PPE can me made easier.

Investigate and clear all discrepancies between the physical count and accounting records.  If necessary, require written explanation from the persons liable.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant request from the depository banks of the Municipality of Madalag the Credit Memorandum, and in case of inaction, the request should be elevated to the Head Office of the bank.


We recommended that Municipal Accountant promptly draw JEVs on all book reconciling items and henceforth, regularly prepare adjustments to those items as it appears in the BRS.

Stale Checks totaling P27,696.83 which have been long outstanding are still appearing in the bank reconciliation statements contrary to Section 59 of the Manual on the NGAS for LGUs, Volume I, thus affecting the correctness of the recorded cash balance in the books.

We recommended the following:

That the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant comply with Section 59 of the Manual on NGAS for LGU Volume 1 pertaining to stale checks and make proper action to effect the necessary adjustments in the books of accounts.
The Municipal Treasurer shall send at once confirmation letters to all payees of checks that remained outstanding to date to determine the actual status of the provincial’s obligation to them and copy furnished this Office of the same.

We recommended that the Accountant exhaust all means for the reconciliation of the Due to Pag-ibig account.  Should it be established that there is overpayment in the premiums, cause the eventual refund from the HDMF and effect the proper adjustment in the books of accounts.
We recommended that Municipal Accounting and Municipal Treasurer’s Offices remit all the taxes withheld intact and within the prescribed period to avoid penalties/surcharges.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer submit the Disbursement Vouchers and their supporting documents and Report of Collections and Deposits and their Official Receipts within the reglementary period to this Office.

We recommended the following:

That for the ensuing year, the municipality plan for 20% DF projects that reflect the priorities in the Annual Investment Program and other local development plans and yet at the same time are eligible under the JMC No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017 of DILG and DBM. Other projects not otherwise eligible should be funded from the regular budget of the municipality.

That the management submit explanation/justification as to why these disbursements were made despite prohibition under Section 3 of JMC 2017-1.

That the programs/projects/activities presented in the annual investment plan be classified in accordance with the classification in Section 3 of JMC 2017-1.

We recommended that the management remind the Petty Cash Custodian to fully liquidate the petty cash and return any balance thereof at the end of each year in accordance with the pertinent rules and regulations.
We recommended that the management exert effort to have its Solid Waste Management Plan for 2018-2027 approved by the National Solid Waste Management Commission.

We recommended that the Solid Waste Management Office continuously coordinate with the concerned Barangays to prioritize and facilitate the establishment of functional MRFs as required under section 32 of Republic Act No. 9003 to help achieve the Municipality’s commitment towards sustainable development and implementation of an environmentally sound and safe management of its solid waste.

We recommended that the provisions of the HGDG be complied with by subjecting the GAD PPAs to the GAD Checklist during the project identification and designing programs to determine whether the same fully address gender related issues.


We recommended that: 

HGDG test be conducted at the end of project implementation and incorporate the results in determining the percentage of PPA expenditures attributable to GAD.

Diligently monitor the implementation of GAD PPAs.

Consider the attribution of GAD PPAs under the agriculture and fisheries, industry, trade and tourism and other sectors identified in the Philippines Plan on GAD.

We recommended the following:

The Inventory Committee conduct an inventory of the local roads under the jurisdiction of the municipality and render a Report on the Physical count of the Road Networks System.

The Municipal General Service Officer or its equivalent:
Render a Report on Local Road Network and Road Map
Regularly maintain and update the Registry of Public Infrastructures for Roads
Keep a complete Local Road Network Property Card for all roads and its components

The Municipal Engineer provide the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal General Service Officer with the complete description and cost segregation of road components for road projects

The Municipal Accountant:
Prepare the JEV to record the beginning balance of the local road network and its components

Prepare and maintain a Local Road Network Ledger Card

Keep and maintain subsidiary records for roads and road components for every road network
Prepare a lapsing schedule for the computation of the depreciation for each component at the end of the year.

We recommended the following:

The reconciliation of the RPCPPE be made by the Municipal Supply/Property Unit and the Accounting Office.  Considering the huge amount and quantity to be reconciled, develop strategies, policies and procedures so that reconciliation of PPE can me made easier in order that the noted discrepancy be minimized, if not eliminated.

Investigate and clear all discrepancies between the physical count and accounting records.  If necessary, require written explanation from the persons liable.
observations.reason: There were items that were still not reconciled.

We recommended the following:

The municipality plan for 20% DF projects that reflect the priorities in the Annual Investment Program and other local development plans and yet at the same time are eligible under the JMC No. 2011-1 dated April 13, 2011 of DILG and DBM. Other projects not otherwise eligible should be funded from the regular budget of the municipality.

The management submit explanation/justification as to why these disbursements were made despite prohibition under Section 4 of JMC 2011-1.
observations.reason: There were items that were charged to the 20% Development Fund that are still in contrary with the JMC.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the management:

Refrain from vaguely naming PPAs in the annual investment plan under the 20% development fund.

Adhere to the provision of Section 5 DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017 to ensure the optimal utilization of 20% development fund to help achieve the desirable socio-economic development and environmental management outcomes of the province.

Mobilize the concerned offices in the early planning and implementation of development projects to avoid incurring possible additional cost as a result of delayed implementation. 

Inform the intended beneficiaries of the programmed projects so proper coordination will facilitate execution of the projects and availment of the benefits thereof at the earliest possible time; and

Submit justifications for the non-implementation and delayed completion of the above-mentioned priority projects totaling P11.693 million.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the following:

The Local Chief Executive directs all the department heads concerned to submit their action plans on the CY 2016 AAR findings and recommendations including that of the prior years, related/connected to their departments/offices and submit the same to the COA Auditor.

The Municipality of Madalag shall faithfully observe the 60-day period within which to submit to the COA from receipt of the succeeding Annual Audit Reports, either in printed form or by way of electronic document, a status report on the actions taken on said audit findings and recommendations.

We recommended that the municipality, through the Sangguniang Bayan, formulate or pass Gender and Development (GAD) Code.  Revisit the provisions of Ordinance No. 005-14 and make amendment(s) or revision(s), whichever is applicable, in order to come up with a feasible for approval GAD Code.  In doing so, a collaboration should be made with the LGU offices/ departments, national government agencies with local presence, women’s organizations and other civil society groups.
observations.reason: The Municipality has no GAD Code, however, it adopted the GAD Code of the Provincial Government.

We recommended that in the planning and budgeting of the SWM of the municipality, make sure that disbursements are investment or capital in nature for those chargeable to 20% DF.  If possible, provide for the ensuing year a separate appropriation for the Solid Waste Management program.



We recommended that the Solid Waste Management Board implement immediately the acquisition of ECO-SWM Site and cause the closure of existing open dump site pursuant to Sections 17(h) and 37 of RA 9003 and closure order of the Department of Environment and Natural Resources.  As an alternative, sanitary landfill sites shall be developed and operated as a final disposal site for solid and, eventually, residual wastes in compliance with the Solid Waste Management Act.

observations.managementAction: Reconciliation of the RPCPPE by the Municipal Supply/Property Unit is still on-going and no corresponding report was rendered to that effect.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the following:

Limit the appropriation of discretionary fund to the prescribed 2% of the total real property tax collections for the next preceding year in conformity with Section 325(h) of R.A. 7160.
Refrain from appropriating separate fund for the same purpose such as miscellaneous expense 
Exercise prudence in the utilization of the discretionary fund.  Ensure that this is use only for the purpose for which it is established and refrain from charging other expenses which should be covered by separate appropriation.

We recommended that payments on infrastructure projects shall be supported with the required documents in accordance with Section 4(6) of PD 1445 and COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012. 

We also recommend that the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) ensure the completeness of bidding documents necessary for the conduct of bid evaluation in conformity with the prescribed procurement procedures set forth under R.A. 9184 and its Revised IRR.

We recommended that for the ensuing year, the municipality plan for 20% DF projects that reflect the priorities in the Annual Investment Program and other local development plans and yet at the same time are eligible under the JMC No. 2011-1 dated April 13, 2011 of DILG and DBM. Other projects not otherwise eligible should be funded from the regular budget of the municipality.
observations.managementAction: The P400,000.00 was realigned to conform with the JMC No. 2017-1

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the municipality take cognizance of the GAD Focal Point System which was created by virtue of Executive Order No. 014, series of 2016 by making sure that the Executive Committees and the Technical Working Group are functional that is efficient in the performance of their functions, roles and responsibilities as embodied therein.



We recommended the following:

1.	The municipality, through its Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator (MPDC), spearhead the setting up of GAD database and the maintenance thereof.  To set up the GAD Database, the Municipality may:

a.	utilize its existing database, planning and data-gathering tools and management information systems to generate gender statistics, age and sex-disaggregated data and other GAD-related information from primary and secondary sources of data such as surveys, studies, focus group discussion (FGD) results, progress reports, monitoring and evaluation results and ODA reports; and 

b.	coordinate with the DILG Field Operation Officer in the generation and processing of sex-disaggregated data and GAD-related information.

2.	The GAD Focal Point System which includes Executive Committees and the technical working group provide technical assistance to the MPDC in ensuring that the database is operational at all times.

We recommended that the municipality, through the Sangguniang Bayan, formulate or pass Gender and Development (GAD) Code.  Revisit the provisions of Ordinance No. 005-14 and make amendment(s) or revision(s), whichever is applicable, in order to come up with a feasible for approval GAD Code.  In doing so, a collaboration should be made with the LGU offices/ departments, national government agencies with local presence, women’s organizations and other civil society groups.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor issue an Executive Order creating the Monitoring and Evaluation Team to perform such functions as enumerated under Section 5 of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01.

He should likewise submit justification/reasons for its non-implementation.
observations.managementAction: There were still PPAs which remained unimplemented.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the SWM Board:

1.	Revise and submit the 10-Year SWM Plan to the NSWMC as required under its Resolution No, 134, series of 2015;
2.	Regularly review and update the SWM Plan to ensure sustainability, viability, effectiveness and relevance in relation to local developments in the fields of solid waste management; and
3.	`Efficiently implement and closely monitor the implementation of the programs, projects and activities of the SWM to make sure that they are implemented as budgeted or planned in a given year.

We recommended that in the planning and budgeting of the SWM of the municipality, make sure that disbursements are investment or capital in nature for those chargeable to 20% DF.  If possible, provide for the ensuing year a separate appropriation for the Solid Waste Management program.



We recommended the following:

1.	The Municipal Solid Waste Management Board be reconstituted to conform to the membership requirement under RA 9003 and its IRR in order to optimize the involvement of civil society as envisioned by law.

2.	The Board regularly convene to enable effective performance of its functions and refrain from delegating its duties and responsibilities to a single person.

We recommended that the Solid Waste Management Board implement immediately the acquisition of ECO-SWM Site and cause the closure of existing open dump site pursuant to Sections 17(h) and 37 of RA 9003 and closure order of the Department of Environment and Natural Resources.  As an alternative, sanitary landfill sites shall be developed and operated as a final disposal site for solid and, eventually, residual wastes in compliance with the Solid Waste Management Act.

We recommended that the MSWM Board faithfully performs its duties and responsibilities on the IEC campaign, specifically the following:

1.	Prepare IEC campaign materials for distribution.  They should be crafted in accord to the level of understanding of the stakeholders in order to saturate the target stakeholders in its entirety;

2.	Develop training modules which should be patterned after those already developed by the Solid Waste Association of the Philippines (SWAPP);

3.	Collaborate IEC campaigns with the Barangay officials to achieve proper coordination and synchronization.  Establish focal persons for each barangays who shall be responsible in the arrangements and schedules of IEC campaigns; and

4.	Follow-ups on IECs be conducted regularly in the whole municipality to ensure that the information and education drive is applied consistently and continuously.

We recommended that Materials Recovery Facility be established in every barangay or cluster of barangays.  The municipality may initially pilot a single barangay to establish MRF project in order to institute a system of knowledge in establishing one as well as to gain commitment/participation of barangay officials on the proper administration and the operation of MRF.  To do so, the plan to establish MRF should be duly back-up with a corresponding budget.

We recommended that the following:

1.	The Sangguniang Bayan enact Municipal Ordinance to enforce the collection of garbage fees in the entire municipality.  Similarly, a Municipal Ordinance declaring certain prohibited acts regarding solid waste disposal and impose penalties to those violators relative to the implementation of the Solid Waste Management Act shall likewise be enacted; and
2.	The Municipal Solid Waste Management Board regularly maintain a report of violators and enforce collection of fines and penalties for their violations.

observations.recommendation: The Inventory Committee come up with the Report on the Physical Count of PPE (RPCPPE) in CY 2015 and every year thereafter and the same shall be reconciled with the books of accounts.  Require the issuance of an Acknowledgement Receipt for Equipment (ARE) for all procured or acquired properties which shall be used to report on the receipt of property and equipment for official use from the Designated Property Officer.

During the planning and budget preparation for the ensuing year, consider and include only expenditures appropriately chargeable against the Special Education Fund pursuant to the provisions of RA 5447, RA 7160 and DECS-DBM-DILG Joint Circular Nos. 01-A and 01-B.

Stop the charges and payment of expenditures for the operations of the district office and schools which are not directly attributable to the establishment and operations of extension classes.

Submit certification from the DepEd Division Superintendent for the necessity and urgency of establishing extension classes.  This documentary evidence must be supported with the computation of the number of pupils/students vis-à-vis the number of regular DepEd teaching staff in the concerned elementary schools.

The management stop the practice of procuring regular office supplies by department/office, instead, require the BAC Secretariat to consolidate   all needed   supplies of departments/offices of the municipality based on their individual Project Procurement Management Plan (PPMP) to the Annual Procurement Plan (APP).  Based on the APP, the BAC should adopt bulk purchases through public bidding to obtain the price most advantageous to the municipality pursuant to RA 9184 and its Revised IRR in order to promote transparency, efficiency and economy in the procurement of the office supplies.

During the planning and budget preparation for the ensuing year, consider and include only expenditures appropriately chargeable against the Special Education Fund pursuant to the provisions of RA 5447, RA 7160 and DECS-DBM-DILG Joint Circular Nos. 01-A and 01-B. Stop the charges and payment of expenditures for the operations of the district office and schools which are not directly attributable to the establishment and operations of extension classes. Submit certification from the DepEd Division Superintendent for the necessity and urgency of establishing extension classes.  This documentary evidence must be supported with the computation of the number of pupils/students vis-à-vis the number of regular DepEd teaching staff in the concerned elementary schools.
observations.reason: There were still inappropriate charges under SEF which we observed and reiterated in this year’s AAR.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the SWM Board: 1. Revise and submit the 10-Year SWM Plan to the NSWMC as required under its Resolution No, 134, series of 2015; 2. Regularly review and update the SWM Plan to ensure sustainability, viability, effectiveness and relevance in relation to local developments in the fields of solid waste management; and 3. Efficiently implement and closely monitor the implementation of the programs, projects and activities of the SWM to make sure that they are implemented as budgeted or planned in a given year. The Municipal Solid Waste Management Board be reconstituted to conform to the membership requirement under RA 9003 and its IRR in order to optimize the involvement of civil society as envisioned by law. The Board regularly convene to enable effective performance of its functions and refrain from delegating its duties and responsibilities to a single person. The Solid Waste Management Board implement immediately the acquisition of ECO-SWM Site and cause the closure of existing open dump site pursuant to Sections 17(h) and 37 of RA 9003 and in compliance with the closure order of the Department of Environment and Natural Resources. The MSWM Board faithfully performs its duties and responsibilities on the IEC campaign, specifically the following: Prepare IEC campaign materials for distribution; Develop training modules which should be patterned after those already developed by the Solid Waste Association of the Philippines (SWAPP); Collaborate IEC campaigns with the Barangay officials to achieve proper coordination and synchronization. Establish focal persons for each barangays who shall be responsible in the arrangements and schedules of IEC campaigns; Follow-ups on IECs be conducted regularly in the whole municipality to ensure that the information and education drive is applied consistently and continuously. Materials Recovery Facility (MRF) be established in every barangay or cluster of barangays. The Sangguniang Bayan enact Municipal Ordinance to enforce the collection of garbage fees in the entire municipality. Similarly, a Municipal Ordinance declaring certain prohibited acts regarding solid waste disposal and impose penalties to those violators relative to the implementation of the Solid Waste Management Act shall likewise be enacted. The Municipal Solid Waste Management Board regularly maintain a report of violators and enforce collection of fines and penalties for their violations.
observations.reason: The municipality’s SWM Board has not been reconstituted to conform to the membership requirement under RA 9003 and its IRR. The Board remained not functional and not being able to regularly convene. The existing disposal facility of the municipality is still an open dumpsite. No IEC campaign materials was prepared nor distributed. Not a single Materials Recovery Facility (MRF) was established. Segregation at source was not observed although during collection, segregation was being observed. Fines and penalties has not be fully enforced as there has no record of violators.

observations.recommendation: The management must firmly require collectors to turnover their collections to the municipal treasurer daily and intact pursuant to Section 29 of the Manual on the NGAS for LGUs, Volume I and Section 26 of COA Circular No.92-382 in order to avoid the possibilities of misuse, misapplication or the ultimate loss of government funds. Collectors who failed to abide with the above stated regulation should be dealt with accordingly. Disciplinary actions or sanctions should be imposed when necessary in order to enforce compliance with the existing rules and regulations on collection, reporting and remittances and in order to safeguard government funds.
observations.reason: Collectors are still delayed in the remittance of their daily collections to the Municipal Treasurer.

observations.recommendation: The management stop the practice of procuring regular office supplies by department/office, instead, require the BAC Secretariat to consolidate all needed supplies of departments/offices of the municipality based on their individual Project Procurement Management Plan (PPMP) to the Annual Procurement Plan (APP). Based on the APP, the BAC should adopt bulk purchases through public bidding to obtain the price most advantageous to the municipality pursuant to RA 9184 and its Revised IRR in order to promote transparency, efficiency and economy in the procurement of the office supplies.
observations.reason: The management is still procuring office supplies per requesting department/office not through bulk prucahses.

We recommended the following:
Limit the appropriation of discretionary fund to the prescribed 2% of the total real property tax collections for the next preceding year in conformity with Section 325(h) of R.A. 7160;
Refrain from appropriating separate fund for the same purpose such as miscellaneous expense; and
Exercise prudence in the utilization of the discretionary fund.  Ensure that this is use only for the purpose for which it is established and refrain from charging other expenses which should be covered by separate appropriation.

We recommended that payments on infrastructure projects shall be supported with the required documents in accordance with Section 4(6) of PD 1445 and COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012. We also recommend that the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) ensure the completeness of bidding documents necessary for the conduct of bid evaluation in conformity with the prescribed procurement procedures set forth under R.A. 9184 and its Revised IRR.

We recommended that for the ensuing year, the municipality plan for 20% DF projects that reflect the priorities in the Annual Investment Program and other local development plans and which are eligible under the JMC No. 2011-1 dated April 13, 2011 of DILG and DBM. Other projects not otherwise eligible should be funded from the regular budget of the municipality.

We recommended that the municipality take cognizance of the GAD Focal Point System which was created by virtue of Executive Order No. 014, series of 2016 by making sure that the Executive Committees and the Technical Working Group are functional that is efficient in the performance of their functions, roles and responsibilities as embodied therein.

We recommended the following:
The Municipality, through its Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator (MPDC), spearhead the setting up of GAD database and the maintenance thereof.  To set up the GAD Database, the Municipality may:
utilize its existing database, planning and data-gathering tools and management information systems to generate gender statistics, age and sex-disaggregated data and other GAD-related information from primary and secondary sources of data such as surveys, studies, focus group discussion (FGD) results, progress reports, monitoring and evaluation results and ODA reports; and
coordinate with the DILG Field Operation Officer in the generation and processing of sex-disaggregated data and GAD-related information.
The GAD Focal Point System which includes Executive Committees and the technical working group provide technical assistance to the MPDC in ensuring that the database is operational at all times.

We recommended that the municipality, through the Sangguniang Bayan, formulate or pass Gender and Development (GAD) Code.  Revisit the provisions of Ordinance No. 005-14 and make amendment(s) or revision(s), whichever is applicable, in order to come up with a feasible for approval GAD Code.  In doing so, a collaboration should be made with the LGU offices/ departments, national government agencies with local presence, women’s organizations and other civil society groups.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor issue an Executive Order creating the Monitoring and Evaluation Team to perform such functions as enumerated under Section 5 of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01.

We recommended that the Municipal Engineer implement and closely monitor the implementation of all the municipal projects to make sure that they are implemented as budgeted or planned in a given year.  He should likewise submit justification/reasons for its non-implementation.

We recommended that the SWM Board:
Revise and submit the 10-Year SWM Plan to the NSWMC as required under its Resolution No, 134, series of 2015;
Regularly review and update the SWM Plan to ensure sustainability, viability, effectiveness and relevance in relation to local developments in the fields of solid waste management; and
Efficiently implement and closely monitor the implementation of the programs, projects and activities of the SWM to make sure that they are implemented as budgeted or planned in a given year.

We recommended that in the planning and budgeting of the SWM of the municipality, make sure that disbursements are investment or capital in nature for those chargeable to 20% DF.  If possible, provide for the ensuing year a separate appropriation for the Solid Waste Management program.

We recommended the following:
The Municipal Solid Waste Management Board be reconstituted to conform to the membership requirement under RA 9003 and its IRR in order to optimize the involvement of civil society as envisioned by law.
The Board regularly convene to enable effective performance of its functions and refrain from delegating its duties and responsibilities to a single person.

We recommended that the Solid Waste Management Board implement immediately the acquisition of ECO-SWM Site and cause the closure of existing open dump site pursuant to Sections 17(h) and 37 of RA 9003 and closure order of the Department of Environment and Natural Resources.  As an alternative, sanitary landfill sites shall be developed and operated as a final disposal site for solid and, eventually, residual wastes in compliance with the Solid Waste Management Act.

We recommended that the MSWM Board faithfully performs its duties and responsibilities on the IEC campaign, specifically the following:
Prepare IEC campaign materials for distribution.  They should be crafted in accord to the level of understanding of the stakeholders in order to saturate the target stakeholders in its entirety.
Develop training modules which should be patterned after those already developed by the Solid Waste Association of the Philippines (SWAPP).
Collaborate IEC campaigns with the Barangay officials to achieve proper coordination and synchronization.  Establish focal persons for each barangays who shall be responsible in the arrangements and schedules of IEC campaigns.
Follow-ups on IECs be conducted regularly in the whole municipality to ensure that the information and education drive is applied consistently and continuously.

We recommended that Materials Recovery Facility be established in every barangay or cluster of barangays.  The municipality may initially pilot a single barangay to establish MRF project in order to institute a system of knowledge in establishing one as well as to gain commitment/participation of barangay officials on the proper administration and the operation of MRF.  To do so, the plan to establish MRF should be duly back-up with a corresponding budget.

We recommended that the following:

Similarly, a Municipal Ordinance declaring certain prohibited acts regarding solid waste disposal and impose penalties to those violators relative to the implementation of the Solid Waste Management Act shall likewise be enacted; and
The Municipal Solid Waste Management Board regularly maintain a report of violators and enforce collection of fines and penalties for their violations.

The Inventory Committee come up with the Report on the Physical Count of PPE (RPCPPE) in CY 2015 and every year thereafter and the same shall be reconciled with the books of accounts. Require the issuance of an Acknowledgement Receipt for Equipment (ARE) for all procured or acquired properties which shall be used to report on the receipt of property and equipment for official use from the Designated Property Officer.
observations.managementAction: The RPCPPE was prepared and submitted by the Inventory Committee
observations.reason: The RPCPPE was not reconciled with the records of the Accounting Office.

observations.recommendation: The Municipal Accountant comply with the prescribed accounting entries on the receipt of fund transfers received from the national government agencies and effect the necessary adjusting journal entries to correct the foregoing misstatement. In the ensuing year, the Municipal Accountant comply with the prescribed accounting entries on the disbursement and liquidation of fund transfers received from the national government agencies taking into account compliance with COA Circular 2015-009 dated December 1, 2015 prescribing the Revised Chart of Accounts for local government units, an utmost consideration must be given in effecting the adjustments.
observations.managementAction: Adjustments were effected by the Municipal Accountant to rectify the noted deficiency.

observations.recommendation: The management, upon authorization from Sanggunian Bayan, open a separate bank account in the General Fund for the 20% Development Fund.  Upon opening, the Municipal Treasurer and Accountant should be directed to transfer the balance of the 20% development fund amounting to P10,897,536.37.
observations.managementAction: The municipality has already open a separate bank account for the 20% Development Fund

observations.recommendation: The management comply strictly with the rules and regulations as provided in Section 89 of PD 1445 and COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997 in the grant, utilization and liquidation of cash advances.  Monitor strictly the liquidation of unliquidated cash advances.
observations.managementAction: The total cash advance balance was liquidated in CY 2016

observations.recommendation: The Petty cash custodian fully liquidate the petty cash fund and remit the remaining balance.  The Municipal Accountant make the necessary entries to record the liquidation of the petty cash fund, upon receipt of the Report of Disbursement supported by the list/summary of PCVs, the PCVs and its supporting documents from the petty cash custodian.
observations.managementAction: A full liquidation was made. The liquidation was already taken up in the books of accounts including the refund of the excess petty cash fund

observations.recommendation: We recommended that payments on infrastructure projects shall be supported with the required documents in accordance with Section 4(6) of PD 1445 and COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012. We also recommend that the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) ensure the completeness of bidding documents necessary for the conduct of bid evaluation in conformity with the prescribed procurement procedures set forth under R.A. 9184 and its Revised IRR.

The municipality transfer the remaining balance of the Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Fund to a special trust fund. Consequently, a Subsidiary Ledger should be maintained by the Municipal Accountant to monitor the yearly transfer of unutilized balance and other receipts of LDRRMF from all sources.
observations.managementAction: The fund was transferred from the General Fund to the Trust Fund.
